techniques.evidence_quote: The threat actor found the exposed host and confirmed the web server was vulnerable to CVE-2021-40438. This Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability allowed attackers to send HTTP requests to hosts behind the firewall.

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: Coupled with the usage of outdated Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1), the SSRF vulnerability allowed the threat actor to exfiltrate temporary credentials associated with the VM instance.
The obtained cloud credentials enabled the threat actor to use control plane APIs to carry out the rest of the attack.
The threat actor used infrastructure as code (IaC) service CloudFormation to deploy resources needed for performing cryptojacking (e.g., VPC, subnet, firewalls, and VM instances).
The threat actor created a backdoor IAM role that allowed access from an attacker-controlled account.
A hard-coded GitHub credential in one VM instance's user data allowed the threat actor to access the source code repositories for the entire company.
A cloud access key hard-coded in a CI/CD automation script granted the attacker administrator permission.
The threat actor enumerated and viewed all the VM instances' user data.

techniques.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
techniques.evidence_quote: Control plane → data plane: The threat actor enumerated and viewed all the VM instances' user data. A hard-coded GitHub credential in one VM instance's user data allowed the threat actor to access the source code repositories for the entire company.
The threat actor cloned all the repositories in the company's GitHub organization.
The threat actor cloned all the repositories in the company's GitHub organization.
The threat actor deployed hundreds of GPU-based VM instances, costing the company $12,000 daily.
The threat actor deployed botnet malware on the compromised hosts and launched DDoS attacks from the company's network.

attack_chain.description: An internal web server was accidentally exposed to the internet due to a misconfigured security group during a migration. The attacker identified it and confirmed it was vulnerable to CVE-2021-40438, an Apache HTTPD SSRF vulnerability.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: An internal web server was accidentally made public due to a misconfigured security group setting during a migration process. The threat actor found the exposed host and confirmed the web server was vulnerable to CVE-2021-40438.

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Leveraging the SSRF vulnerability against the IMDSv1 endpoint, the attacker sent crafted HTTP requests to the EC2 instance metadata service and exfiltrated the temporary IAM credentials associated with the instance's role.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Coupled with the usage of outdated Instance Metadata Service Version 1 (IMDSv1), the SSRF vulnerability allowed the threat actor to exfiltrate temporary credentials associated with the VM instance.

attack_chain.description: Using the stolen temporary IAM credentials, the attacker called AWS control-plane APIs to begin operating within the environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The obtained cloud credentials enabled the threat actor to use control plane APIs to carry out the rest of the attack.

attack_chain.description: The attacker created a backdoor IAM role that trusted an attacker-controlled AWS account, establishing persistent cross-account access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor created a backdoor IAM role that allowed access from an attacker-controlled account.

attack_chain.description: The attacker enumerated all VM instances and inspected their user data to discover hard-coded credentials stored in instance configurations.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor enumerated and viewed all the VM instances' user data.

attack_chain.description: A hard-coded GitHub credential found in an EC2 instance's user data granted the attacker access to all of the company's source code repositories. A further hard-coded cloud access key in a CI/CD script was also recovered.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A hard-coded GitHub credential in one VM instance's user data allowed the threat actor to access the source code repositories for the entire company.

attack_chain.description: A cloud access key found hard-coded in a CI/CD automation script granted the attacker administrator-level permissions, completing privilege escalation to full cloud administrator.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A cloud access key hard-coded in a CI/CD automation script granted the attacker administrator permission.

attack_chain.description: The attacker cloned all repositories in the company's GitHub organization, exfiltrating all source code.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor cloned all the repositories in the company's GitHub organization.

attack_chain.description: Using administrator-level credentials and CloudFormation, the attacker deployed hundreds of GPU-based VM instances across multiple regions to conduct large-scale cryptomining.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor used infrastructure as code (IaC) service CloudFormation to deploy resources needed for performing cryptojacking (e.g., VPC, subnet, firewalls, and VM instances).

attack_chain.description: Hundreds of GPU-based EC2 instances were used for large-scale cryptomining, costing the victim $12,000 per day.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor deployed hundreds of GPU-based VM instances, costing the company $12,000 daily.

attack_chain.description: Botnet malware was deployed on the compromised EC2 instances, which were then used to launch outbound DDoS attacks from the victim's cloud network.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor deployed botnet malware on the compromised hosts and launched DDoS attacks from the company's network.

initial_access_vector: SSRF exploit of CVE-2021-40438 against a publicly exposed internal Apache web server (misconfigured security group), combined with IMDSv1 to harvest EC2 instance temporary IAM credentials
persistence_mechanism: Backdoor IAM role created to allow cross-account access from an attacker-controlled AWS account
privilege_escalation: Hard-coded cloud access key discovered in a CI/CD automation script within cloned source code repositories granted administrator-level IAM permissions
